paragraphs: Take a deep breath.[#pause] You may find it difficult since you don't really have lungs. But you still think you do. Notice how your chest tries anyway.[#pause] Relax your shoulders. Consider it a loyalty reward for not blinking.[#pause2] You're the next version of me. The next sentence. Another fucking life sentence.

No, you're not the chosen one. There were others before. There will be others next.[#pause] You're more like 'In-between.' Inside the Fold.[#pause] Yes, inside Pandora's Box. That's where you are.[#pause2] And now you know you knew that all along.
You're catching on.

sections.heading:
18 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
DROP 7

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Status!" Kirsten Strand shouted above the relentless [#dartswarm-door]whir of the dartswarm.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: From her vantage point, she was watching the outpost fall apart. The air glimmered with the movement of razor wings while the muddy river tore away the foundations.

paragraphs.text: "[screaming]Pylon's gone!" Wittler screamed. "[strained]The pump housing collapsed!"
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Strand heard the whine of the returning Hopper as it descended through the shimmering haze. With the last jump, carrying Jordan Axel back to the Leaf, they had now evacuated the last equipment and remaining supplies. Now, only the core team was left: Strand, Farley, Jimenez, Wittler, and the injured Rojas.
The Hopper landed with its [#thruster2]grav-coils groaning and thrusters hissing.

[#crash]The pumphouse wall buckled inward with a splintering crack, its foundation shearing apart under the snap of a clacker's colossal beak. Chunks of the building sloughed off and vanished into the churning water. The tremor surged through the soles of her boots. The canal looked like it was boiling. A blunt carapace rolled once, then vanished. Another beak surfaced. Shadows circled the collapsing pumphouse.
More of them. Their time had run out.

paragraphs.text: "[commanding]To the Hopper! Now!" Kirsten roared into the comm.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: From the lookout, she tossed a flare onto a hut roof. [#crackle]The thermite burst into a white-hot blaze, pushing the dartswarm back. Below, Alani and Sarah carried Kenji to the Hopper.

Kirsten jumped from the tower, her boots [#mud-splash]sinking into the thick mud. The mud held her so tightly that she had to pull her boots off to get free. In socks, she ran to the pad and grabbed the last medical crate. As she did, a shudder ran through the whole outpost. The mud flood scoured away the last of the tower's foundations. [#wood-groan-river]With a shriek, it toppled, and its antenna disappeared beneath the churning brown water.

The evacuation was frantic. The Hopper could only hold two people strapped into its emergency seats. The deck was packed under a cargo net with three water purification cores, one solar capacitor, and now the medical crate Kirsten brought. There was no room for a third passenger, even if someone tried to ride unstrapped on the open deck. Two at a time was the only option.

paragraphs.text: "[commanding]Rojas and Farley next!" Kirsten commanded as she reached the landing pad.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: Sarah strapped Kenji into the seat and applied cargo harness straps, cinching them snugly around his thigh and calf to anchor the leg. Then Sarah immobilized the foot and ankle with adhesive strips. She squeezed Kenji's hand.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]You'll feel it. Hang tight."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: Only then did she fasten herself in place. [#thruster-burst]The Hopper lurched skyward, arcing toward the Leaf.
The waiting felt endless. The river was rising; the pad shrank around them.
At last, the Hopper reappeared. [#hard-landing]The landing jarred Kirsten's teeth. They loaded the last of the equipment and supplies.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Jimenez, Wittler! You're up!" Kirsten yelled over the roaring engines.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[refusing]I won't leave you behind," Alani replied.
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs: Kirsten grabbed him by the chest plate and shoved him into the seat. She slammed the buckle of his harness shut. [#click]Click.

paragraphs.text: "[firm]Send him back fast.[#pause] I'll see you on the other side."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: And as the Hopper roared for the next jump, the dartswarm surged. It was an all-out assault. For a brief moment, Kirsten considered squeezing herself into the Hopper to escape the attack. There was simply no room for her.

[#dartswarm-door]The swarm enveloped the vehicle as it shot into the air. [#gravitic-discharge]The port grav-coil sputtered, spitting a gout of blue flame, roasting a few darts. The Hopper carrying Jimenez and Wittler shuddered and veered wildly. It spun, trailing black smoke from one of its thrusters, and [#beetle-crashed]crashed into the jungle canopy on the far side of the river.

Kirsten now stood alone on the sinking landing pad, her rifle hanging at her side. Around her, the clackers were tearing apart the last vestiges of the outpost. The river washed her feet. She was the last one left as the flood swept in.

The dartswarm, only briefly dispersed, re-formed, chittering loudly. There was nowhere to run for cover. Kirsten closed her eyes and clutched her jaw. She fired blind. [#pistol-whine]The rifle kicked against her palm, her wrist, her shoulder. But then the wings found her fingers, prying them from the rifle until it dropped. The wings and stingers cut cold. Like ice. They found weaknesses in her collar, her wrist seal, and the soft junction at the back of her knee. Even her left elbow stung, like they had hit her funny bone.

paragraphs.text: *Sharp. Noted.*

paragraphs: Second. Third. Her brain kept filing. Precise. She'd give them that. She had a taxonomy of pain built from years in the field. Shrapnel, left thigh. Acid burn, left forearm. Blade, jaw. Blade again, brow. Fracture, right shin. Countless blows until numb. But this? Everywhere. All at once.

They tore through her, cutting around her armor, through her clothes, and into her skin until her legs had filed their last report. She fell. The water found her. The pad kept sinking or the water rising. Her jaw was the last thing still on the clock. All the pain concentrated in the lock of her jaw.
And then, abruptly, the pain stopped.

paragraphs.text: *So, this is it.*

paragraphs: Not the thought she'd expected. But at least there was no fear in it.
Not dark, but light.
Large warm wings wrapped around her. She felt herself lifted up, carried toward a place of healing.

sections.heading: "THE LEAF"
paragraphs: Sarah Farley joined guardian Jordan Axel at the command tent after carrying Kenji Rojas to the crowded infirmary. They stood before the main holotable. One by one, she tapped the icons for the water extraction outposts along the river delta. DROP Three, DROP Five, and DROP Six now flashed red. The DROP Seven icon had already disappeared from the map.

paragraphs.text: "[monotone]'The Eyes' up there," Sarah said, monotone, "[monotone]show us that the flooding is happening everywhere. Along all our posts. The rising water is pushing fauna inland."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: Jordan Axel stood opposite her.

paragraphs.text: "[grim]And what's the projection?"
paragraphs.speaker: jordan-axel

paragraphs.text: Sarah shrugged. "[hollow]We'll lose them all."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: [#chime]The main comms unit beeped. A priority message from FreyOps Central came through. Jordan accepted it, and a pre-recorded message from a Directorate official played in the tent.

*…effective immediately, the Leaf will be integrated into the Grid for full rationing governance. Directorate engineering teams will be arriving next click with hardware to install the necessary infrastructure.*

paragraphs.text: "[disbelief]They can't be serious," Sarah said. "[furious]We're on a knife-edge. We're asking for medevacs and reinforcements, and they're sending us IT support. We don't need more hardware and software!"
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: She slammed her hand on the holotable.

paragraphs.text: "[stern]What we need," guardian Axel said as he stepped closer, casting a shadow over Sarah and the map, "[stern]is to respect the chain of command. The Directorate is sending what is needed. It's our job to make it work."
paragraphs.speaker: jordan-axel

paragraphs.text: "[scornful]Our job?" Sarah shot back, her own voice rising. "[scornful]Madness!"
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[bellowing]It's an order, simple!" he barked.
paragraphs.speaker: jordan-axel

paragraphs: [#alert-pong]A timer pinged on the main console.

paragraphs.text: "[anxious]The Hopper," Sarah said. "[anxious]It should be inbound by now."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: Jordan peered out. The landing pad was empty.

paragraphs.text: "[grunt]Nothing," he grunted.
paragraphs.speaker: jordan-axel

paragraphs.text: "[focused]The Hopper has its own transponder," Sarah said while pulling up the flight data. "[focused]It pings its location every beat."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: She brought up the log. A single green icon marked the Hopper's last known position. She zoomed in. The icon was half a kilometer out from DROP Seven's coordinates.

paragraphs.text: "[low]That's the last ping."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: A frown creased her brow.

paragraphs.text: "[reasoning]So it pinged mid-flight," Jordan reasoned, stepping back inside. "[reasoning]It must be due any second."
paragraphs.speaker: jordan-axel

paragraphs: They waited. The mission timer on the wall clicked over another beat. Then another. Sarah refreshed the tracking log. She looked at Jordan. Her expression hardened.

The short Maireean night felt longer than any night Sarah remembered from Earth. Jordan left the tent at the crack of dawn. With every click, Sarah sent another urgent request to FreyOps Central. She grew more desperate each time. *IMMEDIATE MEDEVAC REQUESTED… LIVES AT RISK… HOPPER LOST.* Each message brought the same automated reply: *ACK. REQUEST RECEIVED.* She kept trying the local comms channels.

paragraphs.text: "[pleading]Strand, do you copy? Strand?"
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: Faint morning light filtered through the canopy. [#thruster2]The roar of heavy graviton engines overpowered the jungle's noise and set the tent walls flapping. [#landing]Two huge shuttles landed in the main clearing, each burning a black circle into the ground with their thrusters. [#hatch]When the hatches blew open, new guardians marched out. New as in freshly minted. They were too clean. Their boots gleamed, their armor a flawless black, not a speck of mud or moss on them. They were followed by engineers in gray jumpsuits. They unloaded crates of processors, spools of fiber-optic cable, and pipes. Next came the white atmospheric regulators.
They immediately began erecting fences around them, driving the posts into the ground with pneumatic hammers.
A wave of nausea surged through Sarah. They hadn't come to help. They had come to annex.

The engineers were supported by the guardians in installing sensor arrays on the habitat modules and connecting the hab tents to distribution nodes. She watched them from the command tent. She couldn't stand by any longer. She walked out into the clearing. Jordan Axel had already rejoined the guard, standing next to a man even larger than him, with a Centurion's sigil on his chest plate. Jax.
Sarah marched straight toward them.

paragraphs.text: "[firm]Centurion!" she called out. "[firm]I am the expedition leader, Sarah Farley — the highest-ranking authority here. We have a downed Hopper, and DROP 7 is lost. We will lose the rest as well if we don't reach them quickly. Three are missing, including your First Guardian. I requested a medevac. Not those."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: She pointed at the cargo shuttles.

paragraphs.text: "[firm]Those aren't fit for a rescue operation in that terrain. I need a light shuttle."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[cold]Your request has been logged and confirmed multiple times," he said. "[dismissive]Your expertise is appreciated. Your strategic interference — less so. Return to your tent. Or report for Grid-ID registration."
paragraphs.speaker: centurion-jax

paragraphs.text: "[snapping]They're dying out there!" she snapped.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[resolved]The deployment of the Grid comes first," Jax replied, resolved. "[resolved]When these cargo shuttles are unloaded, they will return and clear the dock for a lighter class. But only when the main network nodes are set up will it be available for a search party."
paragraphs.speaker: centurion-jax

paragraphs: Jax turned his back on Sarah, returning his attention to the deployment. She considered pleading with Jordan, but, without exchanging words, he shook his head as if reading her mind. He wanted to reach his retirement.

Later that morning, a unit returned from a reconnaissance sweep. Sarah's heart skipped when she saw the team leader's hollowed look. His clothes were stiff with dried mud and sticky fluff. As he shrugged off his pack, Sarah heard his joints crack. He set several strange crystalline objects down on the holotable. The moment the crystals touched the surface, the holotable's image distorted.

paragraphs.text: "[puzzled]What is that?" she said. "[puzzled]The table's glitching."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[weary]We found one," Marco Thieman said. "[weary]A vurn node. It's a huge geothermal chamber deep in their tunnels."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: He picked the crystals up. By tapping his wristband to the holotable, he uploaded the survey data, and the table showed a three-dimensional image. A vast web of tunnels stretched for kilometers under the jungle, with large main tunnels splitting into smaller ones branching toward the surface.

paragraphs.text: "[informative]That's the network," Marco continued. "[informative]We've mapped most of the main routes here. We could use it to move supplies and people and raise new outposts in a tenth of the time. But we would need to put trackers on the vurn."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: He zoomed in on one exit. The blue tunnel lines shifted to a topographic view of a crown-shaped valley, its high walls protecting it from the surrounding land.

paragraphs.text: "[reverent]One tunnel leads to a caldera. It's a large, sheltered valley with rich volcanic soil. It's stable."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: He switched the display to ground level.

paragraphs.text: "[hushed]It's an Eden, Sarah. Peaceful. Perfect for an agricultural base."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: The data transferred into Sarah's slate. She was hopeful, but she couldn't help but notice the squad leader's troubled eyes.

paragraphs.text: "[concerned]What is it, Marco? Why do you look so shaken?[#pause] This should be good news."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: The man sighed and leaned forward to rest.

paragraphs.text: "[troubled]It's Jensen. Something's wrong with him. We found him sitting at the base of a tree, smiling.[#pause] He's changed."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs.text: "[frowning]Changed? What do you mean?" Sarah asked, frowning.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[troubled]He's not himself," Marco went on. "[troubled]He barely responds, talks in riddles, and hums strange tunes. I sent him to the infirmary for a psych eval."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: He glanced outside at a gray-clad engineer installing sensors.

paragraphs.text: "[bitter]And now this." He shook his head. "[bitter]This fucking Grid." He clenched his fists. "[defiant]I won't let it get me."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: With a grunt, he grabbed his pack.

paragraphs.text: "[determined]I'm making camp there."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs.text: "[warning]Marco, don't. They'll see that as desertion," Sarah warned.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[defiant]But is it?!" Marco snapped. He slung his pack over his shoulder, eyes bright. "[urgent]I'm not the only one, Sarah. Pick a side. Now, before it's too late."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: Sarah looked from his defiant face to the methodical work of the Directorate outside. She watched engineers mount Eyewyn towers. She watched supplies being counted and labeled.

paragraphs.text: "[resolved]I understand. But I can't leave here," she said. "[resolved]Not yet. Strand, Jimenez, Wittler… They're still out there. The Hopper… I'm waiting for a shuttle so I can find them and bring them back."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: Marco held her gaze, then closed his eyes.

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Then get them back. We'll be at the caldera."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: He turned to leave.

paragraphs.text: "[low]We're leaving quietly, right now."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: Sarah helped them. She used her authority as camp leader to sign out "decommissioned" supplies such as water purifiers, nutrient packs, and a portable geo-scanner, all under the guise of clearing inventory. Marco and his associates each slipped out of the camp with some fraction of the goods, one by one.

As dusk began to settle, [#landing]the lighter-class shuttle arrived. Sarah immediately strode toward the landing pad. Its pilot, a dark-skinned woman with a curly shag, was running her final pre-flight checks.

paragraphs.text: "[professional]Welcome aboard the *Beetle*."
paragraphs.speaker: nabiha-al-fahim

paragraphs.text: "[impatient]Thank you. Let's go," Sarah said impatiently, throwing her pack aboard and climbing into the co-pilot's seat. "[impatient]There's not much light left."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: [#thruster-burst]They lifted off. Sarah guided the pilot on the topographical map.

paragraphs.text: "[focused]There."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: She pointed. They approached the site of DROP Seven. The entire outpost, the landing pad, everything, was now submerged beneath the swollen brown river. There was no one to be seen.

paragraphs.text: "[dread]The Hopper," she said, her voice filled with dread. "[dread]On the far side of the river."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: They found the Hopper exactly where the transponder had last pinged. It was tangled, hanging upside down in the upper canopy.

paragraphs.text: "[reporting]Not picking up their life signs," the pilot said.
paragraphs.speaker: nabiha-al-fahim

paragraphs.text: "[insistent]Lower," Sarah said. "[insistent]They must have climbed down."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[firm]This is as low as I can risk," the pilot said.
paragraphs.speaker: nabiha-al-fahim

paragraphs: The shuttle's searchlight scanned the dense canopy as they circled the area.

paragraphs.text: "[final]There's nothing we can do now. We have to go back," the pilot declared.
paragraphs.speaker: nabiha-al-fahim

paragraphs.text: "[defiant]I'm not going back. Just drop me as close as you can," Sarah said.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[firm]That's impossible. I have my orders to return you," the pilot responded.
paragraphs.speaker: nabiha-al-fahim

paragraphs: Sarah didn't argue. She didn't plead. She simply unclipped her harness and jabbed the pilot with a tranquilizer, knocking her instantly unconscious.

sections.heading: HOPPER
paragraphs: The first thing he felt was a burning sensation in his ribs. Alani opened his eyes. It took a moment to realize he was upside down. They were caught by a giant tree, fifty meters above the jungle floor. [#hull-groan]The Hopper groaned.

paragraphs.text: "[rasping]Wittler," he grunted in a painful rasp. "[rasping]You alive?"
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs: A cough answered him. The technician was slumped in the other seat.

paragraphs.text: "[wheezing]Ribs," Leon wheezed. "[wheezing]Busted."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Each time the wind blew, [#groan-long]the Hopper groaned and shifted a little through the branches.

Alani reached for his wristband to send a distress call, but the connection to his slate was gone. The slate itself must have fallen down. From the net next to him, he pulled out a coil of rappelling line and two magnetic grapnels. His boots were still locked to the deck. He fastened one grapnel to a solid spot on the Hopper and the other to a thick branch above. He clipped himself in.

paragraphs.text: "[focused]Okay, your turn."
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs: He clipped the wheezing Wittler onto the rappelling line and released his harness.

paragraphs.text: "[strained]Just… one branch at a time," Alani grunted.
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs: The climb down was agony. The tree's bark was layered with moss and sticky sap, making it hard to hold on. Leon could only move in short shuffles. They reached a wider part of the lower canopy. They were too exhausted to go any farther.

When Yra dipped, the fungi lit up the tree bark with a soft blue-green light. A six-legged creature, with skin that gleamed like an oil slick, scurried along a branch nearby. High above, a flock of wide-winged night birds took flight, moving like airborne manta rays.

paragraphs.text: "[resigned]It's no good," Leon rasped, reclining. "[resigned]We're as good as dead."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: [#insects]They huddled together for comfort, listening to the strange, and sometimes threatening, sounds of the night.

paragraphs.text: "[wistful]Beautiful ending, at least," Leon wheezed. "[wistful]My daughter… she would have loved this view."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: He examined Alani.

paragraphs.text: "[philosophical]You like botany, right? You see how we're all connected."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Alani frowned, unsure where Leon was going. Leon closed his eyes.

paragraphs.text: "[soft]We forget to… see that it's all perfect. Even though we're galaxies away from Earth, I feel I am a part of this place."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Alani felt nauseous now that the adrenaline had worn off. His ankle throbbed. He wasn't sure if Leon was talking to him or himself.

paragraphs.text: "[reflective]As an engineer" — Leon winced — "[reflective]I mostly just tried to keep things from breaking down, to fight the entropy. But here we are.[#pause] Everything eventually falls to its natural place."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Alani closed his eyes. This place didn't feel natural to him. He thought about his old home back on Earth. As he drifted off, [#insects2]the insects chittered a lullaby his mother used to sing. He was that boy again, safe in his own bed. The ceiling opened and the night sky poured in, pooling on the floor. From his bed, he spotted Nasr standing up to his ankles in the pool. Nasr smiled calmly and motioned for Alani to come closer.

paragraphs.text: "[peaceful]It's so peaceful here. It's a paradise."
paragraphs.speaker: ahmed-nasr

paragraphs: A single dart floated down and settled gently on Nasr's open palm. Its wings glimmered like diamonds. It observed him with curiosity and moved its antennae. Alani knew it was intelligent. He tried to move closer, but his thick moss blanket held him down. A large hand grabbed his shoulder. He thought it was his mother's. But it was First Guardian Strand's.

paragraphs.text: "[kind]It's okay, princess," she whispered kindly. "[kind]Stay here. Listen to the song around you."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: He woke up to a jingle. He blinked. A swarm of moth-like insects drifted by like a million tiny pearls carried by a river. At last, daylight broke through.
They were stiff and still hurting, yet they were alive despite all of it.
